title: funky crispy greek stifado
description: a hearty, comforting vegetarian stifado dish with a crispy twist, perfect for a sick day or a cozy evening in, featuring sweet and sour flavors of the greek cuisine.
instructions: heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a large tandoor or oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat.
add the sliced onion and cook until caramelized, stirring occasionally.
add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
add the sliced carrots and zucchini and cook for 3-4 minutes, stirring occasionally.
add the sliced red bell pepper and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, stirring occasionally.
stir in the tandoor spice mix, honey, apple cider vinegar, salt, and black pepper.
add the canned crushed tomatoes and stir to combine.
bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally.
stir in the pitted kalamata olives.
serve the stifado hot, garnished with fresh parsley or dill, if desired.
to make the crispy topping, heat the remaining 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a small skillet over medium-high heat.
add 1/4 cup of breadcrumbs and cook until crispy and golden brown, stirring constantly.
sprinkle the crispy breadcrumbs over the stifado and serve immediately.
